summary: Hierarchical Inspection Structure - All features tested and verified. Backend: 13/13 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All UI elements verified - Entry cards with Resource/Date/Crates header, Rejection items sub-grid with Count/Reason, Add Entry and Add Rejection buttons, Totals bar, Rejection Summary table with nested entries expanded to flat rows, Activity Log with per-entry nested details.

issues: Console warning: 'Select is changing from uncontrolled to controlled' - minor React state management issue in Select components

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: Select components show 'uncontrolled to controlled' warning - consider initializing with empty string instead of undefined

success_rate.frontend: 100% - All UI elements and integrations verified

seed_data_creation: Used existing test batch: BATCH-TEST-001 (ID: 5833eb23-8664-4a53-8fc7-eedb9ff18178). Created test QC team members and rejection reasons during tests (cleaned up after).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Hierarchical Inspection Structure fully tested. Key changes from previous iteration: 1) Data model changed from flat grid to hierarchical: Entry (Resource + Date + Crates) → multiple Rejection Items (Count + Reason). 2) Backend payload: {stage_id, entries: [{resource_id, resource_name, date, qty_inspected, rejections: [{qty_rejected, reason}]}], remarks}. 3) Validation: total crates from all entries cannot exceed pending, per-entry rejected total cannot exceed entry's crates × bottles_per_crate. 4) Frontend data-testids: entry-card-{idx}, entry-resource-{idx}, entry-date-{idx}, entry-crates-{idx}, entry-remove-{idx}, add-entry-{stage_id}, rej-item-{eIdx}-{rIdx}, rej-qty-{eIdx}-{rIdx}, rej-reason-{eIdx}-{rIdx}, rej-remove-{eIdx}-{rIdx}, add-rej-{eIdx}, insp-submit-{stage_id}. 5) Rejection Summary table expands entries[].rejections[] into flat rows with columns: Resource, Date, Stage, Crates Inspected, Rejected Count, Passed, Reason, Remarks. 6) Activity Log shows per-entry nested details with resource name, date, crates, and rejection breakdown.
